sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Dr Pepper: Health trends challenge soda dominance amid Keurig growth
feed.summary: Dr Pepper, despite its strong market share as America's second-best-selling soda, faces challenges from shifting consumer preferences towards healthier alternatives. Its parent company, Keurig Dr Pepper, navigates these trends by maintaining a diversified portfolio of beverages and innovating with products like the Zero Sugar line. The company's strategy balances traditional soda appeal with new market demands, aiming to sustain growth amidst competition and health-conscious consumer shifts.

feed.title: EQT Declares Quarterly Dividend of $0.165 per Share

feed.title: Delta Air Lines Expands Atlanta to Caribbean Routes for 2026 Winter, Boosting Antigua, St. Kitts, Curaçao Travel Demand
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines is significantly expanding its seasonal winter routes from Atlanta to Caribbean destinations like Antigua, St. Kitts, Curaçao, Bonaire, and St. Croix for 2026, driven by exploding leisure travel demand. Atlanta, Delta's largest hub, serves as a central gateway, leveraging its extensive domestic feeder traffic and operational efficiency. This strategic expansion focuses on weekend and seasonal flights to maximize profitability and caters to the growing trend of leisure travel dominating the aviation industry.

feed.title: Titan Promotes Kendra Mann to Director of Aftermarket Sales for Titan/Goodyear Ag
feed.summary: Titan International has promoted Kendra Mann to Director of Aftermarket Sales for Titan/Goodyear Ag, where she will be responsible for strategy and execution of agricultural tire products in the aftermarket channel. Mann brings over 20 years of tire industry experience, including eight years at Titan, to this role. She will work with her field sales team, product management, and dealer partners to enhance alignment and customer responsiveness within the channel.

feed.title: JPMorgan Initiates ResMed (RMD) With “Overweight” Rating and A$37.6 PT
feed.summary: JPMorgan has initiated coverage of ResMed Inc. (RMD) with an "Overweight" rating and an A$37.60 price target, despite growing investor focus on GLP-1 therapies for sleep apnea. The firm believes GLP-1 treatments, while clinically beneficial, will not diminish the long-term investment case for continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) therapy. ResMed management also anticipates that GLP-1 therapies and consumer sleep-tracking wearables will increase awareness and enrollment in sleep apnea diagnosis and treatment, ultimately acting as a tailwind for the company's demand pipeline.

feed.title: Apple Tops Smartphone Sales in Early 2026 Amid Market Decline - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Apple Inc. became the leading global smartphone vendor in Q1 2026, despite an overall market contraction, owing to strong demand for iPhones across multiple international markets and effective trade-in programs. Analysts attribute this success to significant consumer demand, an extensive installed base of older iPhones signaling future replacement demand, and strategic component sourcing to protect its market position.

Despite competition, Thermo Fisher's comprehensive ecosystem and strategic partnerships position it for continued growth in the expanding life sciences and materials science sectors.

feed.title: Mastercard, FinVolution launch Luvit Card to expand digital credit access in PH
feed.summary: Mastercard and FinVolution Group have partnered to launch the Luvit Card, aiming to expand digital credit access in the Philippines. This new card offers installment-based credit to Filipinos who may not have access to traditional credit products, facilitating purchases across Mastercard's global merchant network. The Luvit Card, available virtually and physically, is integrated into the Luvit app for a streamlined application and payment experience.

feed.title: Is Cheaper Energy and Shipping Altering The Investment Case For Charles River Laboratories (CRL)?
feed.summary: The reopening of the Strait of Hormuz has led to reduced global logistics and energy costs, which could benefit Charles River Laboratories International by easing operating conditions and supporting margins for its research and lab services. While helpful, these cost reductions are not considered "game-changing," and the company still faces risks from demand softness and cancellations in longer-duration studies. Investors will closely watch the upcoming Q1 2026 results for insights into how these lower costs translate to revenue and earnings, alongside broader cost initiatives.

feed.title: Equifax Bonitätsprüfung: Why Credit Checks Matter More in Volatile Markets
feed.summary: As economic uncertainty and volatile markets increase, Equifax's Bonitätsprüfung (credit check) service becomes even more crucial for individuals, businesses, and lenders. The service provides precise credit assessment, helping to mitigate lending risks and stabilize markets through its vast data aggregation, AI-driven insights, and adherence to global data privacy laws like GDPR. While facing competition from other bureaus and fintechs, Equifax maintains its market edge through comprehensive, verified data, strategic international growth, and investments in cybersecurity and cloud infrastructure, positioning it for continued relevance despite potential economic downturns or regulatory challenges.

feed.title: Deere Settled for $99 Million Over Repair Costs. Farmers Are Still Worried.
feed.summary: Deere & Company has settled for $99 million over repair costs, but farmers remain concerned about the company's tight control over repairs and the rising expenses of maintaining complex farm equipment. Farmers, like Nebraska's Guy Mills who has spent over half a million dollars on repairs, are engaging in legal battles to address these issues. The settlement will allow farmers to seek reimbursements for repair costs.

feed.title: Inside Blackstone: Stephen A. Schwarzman on Long-Term Investing Across Cycles

Schwarzman also shares insights on the opportunities in private markets, the transformative impact of AI, and Blackstone's strategic focus on the Asia Pacific region.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges ImmunityBio, Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against ImmunityBio, Inc. (NASDAQ: IBRX) and certain officers, alleging federal securities law violations. The lawsuit claims that ImmunityBio's CEO, Soon-Shion, materially overstated the capabilities of Anktiva, and that the company's statements about its business, operations, and prospects were false and misleading during the period of January 19, 2026, to March 24, 2026. Investors who purchased ImmunityBio securities during this period are encouraged to join the case and have until May 26, 2026, to request to be appointed as lead plaintiff.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges ImmunityBio, Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
